title: regression: podman run: exit status is 0 after signal
body:
Possibly a zombie of #389; I'm filing a new issue because that was closed a long time ago, hence this may actually be a new cause for the same behavior.

In window 1:
```
# podman run --rm --name foo fedora sleep 60;echo $?
```
In a second window: `podman kill foo` or `podman stop foo`. Back to the first window, the exit status is `0` (expected: 137).

podman-0.8.1-1.git6b4ab2a.fc28

comments.body: I feel like we might be having Conmon issues in the new 0.8.1 builds - will take a look after lunch
Yeah, it's starting to look like conmon. I'm seeing old problems with `podman attach` too (#580)
This is a packaging issue - the latest build of Podman for F27 looks to have dropped its built-in `conmon` to use the (far too old) system `conmon`

^^^ F28, not F27: f27 actually still includes conmon:

```
f27]# rpm -ql podman|grep conm
/usr/libexec/podman/conmon
```

```
f28# rpm -ql podman|grep conm
f28#
```

f27 has its own issues.
What I'm getting out of this is that our builds are really broken right now.

podman on F28 relies on the installed conmon package. I just pushed cri-o into testing could you test with the cri-o 1.11 package?

cri-o-1.11.1-1.git1759204.fc28
conmon-1.11.1-1.git1759204.fc28.aarch64.rpm

I couldn't find that build in any repo including updates-testing; but I downloaded conmon from [koji](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=1104940). Looks good. Someone please remember to update the conmon dependency in the podman specfile.

This should be fixed on our end - we're back on a good conmon version
@edsantiago Feel free to reopen if it's not
